title: [InstCombine] Propagate NUW flags for `shl (lshr X, C1), C2 -> shl X, C2-C1`
summary:
The developers generalized the optimization pattern by extending it to handle both exact and non-exact right shifts, while preserving overflow flags more accurately. Originally, the transformation only applied to `lshr` followed by `shl nsw`, converting `(X >> C1) << C` to `X << (C - C1)` when `C1 < C`. The patch adds logic to propagate `nsw` from the original `shl` to the new `shl`'s `nuw` flag when the right shift is `lshr`. This ensures that the transformation remains valid for exact shifts (`lshr exact`) and maintains the same overflow semantics.

The key insight is that `shl nsw` combined with `lshr` guarantees no unsigned wrap in the resulting shift, because the right shift clears high bits, preventing overflow when left-shifted back. The patch conditionally sets `nuw` on the new `shl` if the original had `nsw` and the right shift is `lshr`, preserving safety. This generalization broadens applicability to more patterns without introducing new instructions, maintaining correctness and performance.
